macro.source: reused TARGET 07.395 exact split-K macro, same variant, TP8, page_size=256, num_pages=128

top_five_bottlenecks.interpretation: largest exact-path kernel bucket after split-K; dominated by PyTorch copy/cat/index/gather/fill style graph nodes
legacy sparse CUDA kernel is prefill/extend only, not decode split-K
many small graph-node elementwise/reduction kernels around exact projection, normalization, and staging
bf16 indexer logits/topk/cache preparation remains material, especially in prefill
selective projection FP8 GEMM from the current exact stack; this is not packed FP8 KV/indexer cache

graph_overhead.interpretation: node trace inflates cudaGraphLaunch API duration; use non-node value for overhead and node trace for graph-kernel attribution

decision: Run TARGET 07.41 next. The top remaining exact bottleneck is the indexer/cache/runtime/copy cluster plus legacy prefill sparse, not decode split-K and not yet a pure packed FP8 KV/indexer cache precision/layout decision.
do_not_continue_condition: Do not continue split-K sparse decode polish unless a fresh profile makes decode split-K gather/split/combine a top-two contributor again.
